name: lookup_entry
description:
Looks up detailed technical metadata of a single Entry.

This method returns the raw structured `google.cloud.dataplex.v1.Entry` message, including its schema and attached aspects.

**When to use `LookupEntry` vs `LookupContext`**:
- Use **`LookupContext`** (recommended for general LLM reasoning) when you need a pre-formatted, easy-to-read YAML summary of the resource's metadata, schema, and quality to answer user questions or plan queries.
- Use **`LookupEntry`** when you need to programmatically inspect the raw structure, retrieve specific raw aspect payloads, or prepare to update aspects.

**Performance Tip**:
- Avoid retrieving `ALL` aspects if you only need specific information. Use the `CUSTOM` view and specify the aspects you need in `aspect_types` (e.g., `contacts`, `overview`) to minimize latency and payload size.

parameters.description: Lookup Entry request in the simplified MCP format.

aspectTypes.description: Optional. Limits the returned aspects to these specific types. Only effective when `view` is set to `CUSTOM`. Supported system aspect types include: - `contacts` (ownership/stewardship) - `data-quality-scorecard` (data quality status) - `data-profile` (technical profiling stats) - `refresh-cadence` (contracts) - `overview` (documentation) - `generic` (custom generic metadata)

entry.description: Required. The resource name of the entry to look up. Must be in the format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/entryGroups/{entry_group_id}/entries/{entry_id}` Example for a BigQuery table: `projects/{project_id_or_number}/locations/{location}/entryGroups/@bigquery/entries/bigquery.googleapis.com/projects/{project_id}/datasets/{dataset_id}/tables/{table_id}` This is the same value returned in the `dataplexEntry.name` field of the `SearchEntries` response.

location.description: Required. The location to which the request should be attributed.

paths.description: Optional. Limits the aspects returned to those associated with the provided paths within the Entry. It only works for CUSTOM view.

projectId.description: Required. The project ID to which the request should be attributed.

view.description: Optional. Controls which parts of the entry are returned. - `ENTRY_VIEW_UNSPECIFIED` / `FULL`: Returns the entry and keys of all aspects. - `BASIC`: Returns only the core entry metadata (no aspects). Fast and lightweight. - `CUSTOM`: Returns only the aspects specified in `aspect_types` or `paths`. Highly recommended when you only need specific metadata (like contacts or quality). - `ALL`: Returns the entry and all attached aspects with their full content.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Returns all required aspects as well as the keys of all non-required aspects.
Returns aspects matching custom fields in GetEntryRequest. If the number of aspects exceeds 100, the first 100 will be returned.
Returns all aspects. If the number of aspects exceeds 100, the first 100 will be returned.
